Name: Product Goal
Description: Build a secure, cloud-deployed leadership portal for a new safehouse nonprofit that helps staff manage resident care, helps administrators track operations and donations, and helps donors understand anonymized impact through reporting, analytics, and machine learning insights.

Name: Monday Sprint Goal
Description: Understand the problem, users, and data well enough to create a complete backlog, clear priorities, and a realistic build plan for the week.

Name: Tuesday Sprint Goal
Description: Choose the final design direction and build the public-facing, authentication, and security foundations of the application.

Name: Wednesday Sprint Goal
Description: Deliver and deploy a working end-to-end slice of the application with real database persistence and core portal functionality.

Name: Thursday Sprint Goal
Description: Complete advanced case-management, analytics, ML, security, accessibility, and responsiveness work so the project is ready for final demo and submission.

Name: PB1 - Define Team Roles and Working Agreement
Description: Point estimate: 1. Assign the Scrum Master and Product Owner, agree on team norms, decide branch workflow expectations, and clarify who owns each major workstream.

Name: PB2 - Create Personas and Journey Map
Description: Point estimate: 3. Build two realistic primary personas, document their goals and pain points, and map their journey so design and feature decisions stay tied to user needs.

Name: PB3 - Write Problem Statement and Product Goal
Description: Point estimate: 1. Define the core customer problem, the desired business outcome, and the product goal that will guide backlog prioritization.

Name: PB4 - Build MoSCoW Prioritization
Description: Point estimate: 2. Convert requirements into Must, Should, Could, and Won't categories, add team-generated nice-to-have ideas, and document one feature intentionally not built.

Name: PB5 - Set Up Product Backlog and Sprint Boards
Description: Point estimate: 3. Create the master backlog in Trello, estimate work, assign ownership, and split work into Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday sprint backlogs.

Name: PB6 - Create Burndown Chart and Daily Tracking
Description: Point estimate: 2. Set up a burndown chart and update it throughout the week so the team can monitor progress and adjust priorities.

Name: PB7 - Produce Key Wireframes
Description: Point estimate: 3. Design wireframes for the three most important screens in desktop view to establish the layout and feature priorities before implementation.

Name: PB8 - Explore and Choose UI Direction
Description: Point estimate: 3. Generate multiple AI-assisted UI concepts, evaluate them, document feedback, choose a final direction, and record the changes made from the original concepts.

Name: PB9 - Define Branding and Product Naming Direction
Description: Point estimate: 1. Choose or defer the organization name, align visual tone and messaging, and ensure placeholder branding stays consistent until final branding is selected.

Name: PB10 - Profile the Raw CSV Dataset
Description: Point estimate: 3. Inspect the 17 source CSV files, understand table relationships, identify missing values and inconsistencies, and document which fields and tables will actually be used.

Name: PB11 - Design the Relational Database Model
Description: Point estimate: 3. Decide how the provided CSV structure maps into the app database, including keys, relationships, normalization choices, and any additional fields or tables needed.

Name: PB12 - Build Data Import and Seeding Strategy
Description: Point estimate: 3. Create the approach for loading raw data into the database, including seed and demo data, environment-specific setup, and a repeatable process for local and deployed environments.

Name: PB13 - Implement Data Pipeline for Source Data Preparation
Description: Point estimate: 5. Clean, transform, and prepare the operational, donor, and outreach data so it can support the application and ML workflows reliably.

Name: PB14 - Build Backend Project Configuration
Description: Point estimate: 2. Configure the ASP.NET Core API, database connection, environment variables, dependency setup, and application startup behavior.

Name: PB15 - Implement Authentication and Password Policy
Description: Point estimate: 3. Configure ASP.NET Identity login with the class-required password policy, lockout behavior, JWT token handling, and secure authentication flow.

Name: PB16 - Implement Role-Based Access Control
Description: Point estimate: 3. Enforce authorization so Admin can create, update, and delete data, Employee has restricted access, and Donor only sees donor-appropriate data.

Name: PB17 - Build User and Seed Account Setup
Description: Point estimate: 2. Create seeded Admin, Employee, and Donor accounts for testing and grading, and make sure roles are correctly assigned and demonstrated.

Name: PB18 - Build Public Landing Page
Description: Point estimate: 3. Create a polished public homepage that introduces the mission, builds trust, and gives users clear next steps such as viewing impact or signing in.

Name: PB19 - Build Public Impact Dashboard
Description: Point estimate: 3. Create a public-facing dashboard showing anonymized, aggregated impact data in a clear, donor-friendly format.

Name: PB20 - Build Login Experience
Description: Point estimate: 3. Implement the login page with validation, helpful error handling, session behavior, and appropriate redirects for authenticated users.

Name: PB21 - Build Privacy Policy and Cookie Consent
Description: Point estimate: 2. Publish a privacy policy tailored to the project and implement a functional cookie consent experience consistent with course expectations.

Name: PB22 - Build Shared Frontend Layout and Navigation
Description: Point estimate: 3. Create consistent layout components, navigation, footer, protected route handling, and app-wide styling so the experience feels unified.

Name: PB23 - Build Admin Dashboard
Description: Point estimate: 3. Create a command-center dashboard for staff and admins showing high-level operational metrics such as active residents, donation activity, and upcoming case work.

Name: PB24 - Build Donor and Supporter CRM
Description: Point estimate: 5. Allow staff to search, view, create, edit, and manage supporter records across donor and supporter types, including active and inactive status.

Name: PB25 - Build Contribution Tracking
Description: Point estimate: 5. Support recording and reviewing monetary, in-kind, time, skill-based, and advocacy contributions tied to supporters.

Name: PB26 - Build Donation Allocation Tracking
Description: Point estimate: 3. Show how donations are allocated across safehouses and program areas so leadership can connect giving to operational outcomes.

Name: PB27 - Build Donor Portal
Description: Point estimate: 3. Provide donors with access to their own giving history and an anonymized summary of the impact their support contributes to.

Name: PB28 - Build Resident Intake and Profile Management
Description: Point estimate: 5. Implement resident records with demographic, case, family, referral, admission, and reintegration fields aligned to the case requirements.

Name: PB29 - Build Searchable Caseload Inventory
Description: Point estimate: 5. Provide staff with filtering and searching tools for residents based on status, safehouse, case category, risk, and social worker assignment.

Name: PB30 - Build Process Recording Module
Description: Point estimate: 5. Implement chronological counseling session entry and review, including emotional states, interventions, narrative summaries, and follow-up actions.

Name: PB31 - Build Home Visitation Module
Description: Point estimate: 5. Support entry and review of home visit details such as visit type, observations, family cooperation, safety concerns, and follow-up needs.

Name: PB32 - Build Case Conference Tracking
Description: Point estimate: 3. Record case conference history, upcoming conferences, and decisions related to resident planning and progress.

Name: PB33 - Build Reports and Analytics
Description: Point estimate: 5. Create aggregated operational reports for donations, resident outcomes, safehouse comparisons, service delivery, and reintegration trends.

Name: PB34 - Add OKR Metric to the App
Description: Point estimate: 2. Select one meaningful success metric, display it in the application, and explain why it best represents value for the organization.

Name: PB35 - Build Social Media Analytics
Description: Point estimate: 4. Analyze social media post performance, engagement, donation referrals, and campaign effectiveness to support smarter outreach decisions.

Name: PB36 - Build ML Notebook Pipelines
Description: Point estimate: 5. Create the required Jupyter notebooks for donor, resident, outreach, and forecasting insights using the provided and derived data.

Name: PB37 - Export ML Predictions for App Use
Description: Point estimate: 3. Generate prediction outputs from the notebooks in a consistent format that can be imported into the application.

Name: PB38 - Build ML Import and API Support
Description: Point estimate: 3. Create or finalize backend support for importing ML outputs, storing predictions, and exposing them through secure API endpoints.

Name: PB39 - Build ML Insights and Action Center Pages
Description: Point estimate: 3. Display ML results in the app so staff can review prediction types, risks, and suggested next actions in a usable format.

Name: PB40 - Implement Input Validation and Error Handling
Description: Point estimate: 3. Validate user input on the frontend and backend and ensure the app fails gracefully with helpful, safe error messages.

Name: PB41 - Implement Delete Confirmation and Data Integrity Safeguards
Description: Point estimate: 2. Require confirmation before deletions, protect restricted operations, and ensure that changes to sensitive data only happen through authorized workflows.

Name: PB42 - Implement Secrets and Credential Safety
Description: Point estimate: 2. Keep secrets out of source control, configure environment variables correctly, and document safe credential handling practices for the team.

Name: PB43 - Verify HTTPS and HTTP-to-HTTPS Security
Description: Point estimate: 2. Ensure deployed services use TLS correctly and redirect insecure traffic to HTTPS as required by the grading rubric.

Name: PB44 - Improve Accessibility Across All Pages
Description: Point estimate: 3. Fix semantics, labels, contrast, keyboard access, and screen-reader usability so every page can reach the required Lighthouse accessibility score.

Name: PB45 - Improve Mobile Responsiveness Across All Pages
Description: Point estimate: 3. Make every page resize and function well across desktop and mobile layouts, including forms, tables, navigation, and dashboards.

Name: PB46 - Test Core User Flows End-to-End
Description: Point estimate: 3. Verify that public users, admins, employees, and donors can complete their key tasks successfully in the deployed environment.

Name: PB47 - Deploy Database, API, and Frontend
Description: Point estimate: 5. Deploy the relational database, ASP.NET backend, and React frontend to the cloud and confirm that environment variables and connectivity work correctly.

Name: PB48 - Prepare Screenshots, Evidence, and Video Proof
Description: Point estimate: 3. Collect screenshots, security evidence, page demos, deployment proof, and other artifacts needed to clearly show completion in the grading videos.

Name: PB49 - Gather User Feedback and Iterate
Description: Point estimate: 2. Show the current product to a real user, record at least five actionable pieces of feedback, and make targeted improvements based on what is learned.

Name: PB50 - Complete Final Retrospective and Submission Prep
Description: Point estimate: 2. Finish the retrospective, summarize team contributions, confirm deliverables are complete, and prepare the repo, links, and presentation materials for submission.

Name: PB51 - Create Tech Stack Diagram
Description: Point estimate: 1. Build the required frontend, backend, and database architecture diagram with the chosen technology logos for the Tuesday deliverable.

Name: PB52 - Capture UI Exploration Evidence
Description: Point estimate: 3. Save three screenshots of each of three UI design options, record five AI feedback questions for each, summarize the takeaways, and document the chosen direction.

Name: PB53 - Capture Sprint and Page Screenshot Evidence
Description: Point estimate: 2. Collect sprint backlog screenshots for each day and current-state screenshots of at least five pages in both desktop and mobile views.

Name: PB54 - Prepare Security and Feature Video Checklist
Description: Point estimate: 2. Create a checklist so the final video clearly demonstrates HTTPS, redirects, auth, RBAC, delete confirmation, privacy and cookie behavior, deployed pages, and ML integration.

Name: PB55 - Deliver One Working Deployed Page
Description: Point estimate: 3. Ensure at least one page is fully deployed to the cloud and persists real data to the database for the Wednesday milestone.
